workoutList.description:
Endurance run in Z2 (keep both pace/power AND heart rate in Z2).

This run should be controlled and fairly comfortable but it shouldn't be a shuffle. Do the "talk test" (try talking to yourself and see if you can talk in complete sentences without huffing and puffing).

Session-RPE for the run should be 3-5

Warm-up:
-20' easy, gradually getting towards the high end of Z2. Include 4 x 30" builds to FTP or higher.

Main set - if you can, do as much as possible in the aerobars if training for a non-draft race (but you can take short breaks from the TT-position and sit up every now and then if needed):
3 x (
-15 minutes @ 85-95% of FTP.
-5 minutes easy spin
)

-Easy riding in Z2 or Z1 to prescribed duration.

------------------------------------------------------

-Session-RPE for the workout should be 6-8/10

-Be well fuelled and hydrated coming into the workout. And fuel and hydrate adequately during the workout.

Very easy ride in Z1 or low end of Z2. Don't let heart rate exceed Z2 at any point. For power, take the midpoint of Z2 as your cap. Going harder will not make the ride any better/more beneficial, so hold yourself back!

Session-RPE for the workout should be 1-3/10.

The purpose of the ride is simply to collect more aerobic cycling volume with an absolute minimal impact on tissue (muscle, tendon and bone) and on the central nervous system.

Very easy ride in Z1 or low end of Z2. Don't let heart rate exceed Z2 at any point. For power, take the midpoint of Z2 as your cap. Going harder will not make the ride any better/more beneficial, so hold yourself back!

Session-RPE for the workout should be 1-3/10.

The purpose of the ride is simply to collect more aerobic cycling volume with an absolute minimal impact on tissue (muscle, tendon and bone) and on the central nervous system.

Warm-up:
-20' easy, gradually getting towards the high end of Z2. Include 4 x 15" fast strides.

Main set:
-30 minutes @ Z3 (based on pace/power). Depending on how you feel on the day this might be the very high end of Z3 or somewhere in the low end or midrange of the zone.

-Easy running in Z2 or Z1 to prescribed duration. Include another 4 x 15" fast strides with at least 90 seconds between strides through this segment.

------------------------------------------------------

-You choose whether to run this workout on flat roads, undulating or hilly roads, or even on a track for perfect pace control. If running on undulating terrain, pace may not be very useful, so rely on power/HR/RPE instead.

-Session-RPE for the workout should be 6-8/10

Very easy run in Z1 or low end of Z2. Don't let heart rate exceed Z2 at any point. For pace/power, take the midpoint of Z2 as your cap. Going faster will not make the run any better/more beneficial, so hold yourself back!

Session-RPE for the workout should be 1-3/10.

The purpose of the run is simply to collect some running volume with an absolute minimal impact on tissue (muscle, tendon and bone) and on the central nervous system.

workoutList.workoutName: Greased Lightning (includes option for Zwift race)
workoutList.description:
Warm-up:
-30' easy, gradually getting towards the high end of Z2. Include 4 x 30" builds to FTP or higher.

Main set - feel free to do this workout sitting up. Hitting the prescribed power targets is the main priority here, not specificity of bike position:

8 x (
-1' @ 115-120% FTP, 60-70 rpm
-30" passive rest! (or less than 80W)
)
-6' easy spin

8 x (
-1' @ 115-120% FTP, 95+ rpm
-30" passive rest! (or less than 80W)
)
-6' easy spin

8 x (
-1' @ 115-130% FTP, choice cadence (if you can hit 130% here, go for it)
-30" passive rest! (or less than 80W)
)
-6' easy spin

-Easy riding in Z2 to prescribed duration.

------------------------------------------------------

-Session-RPE for the workout should be 8-9/10

-Be well fuelled and hydrated coming into the workout. And fuel and hydrate adequately during the workout.

Main set - note cadence prescriptions! You can do this workout sitting up if you find that it's difficult to pedal smoothly at low rpm:s when in the aerobars.

3 x (
-10' @ 60-65% FTP, choice rpm
-25' @ 70-75% FTP, 50-60 rpm (important!!!)
)

------------------------------------------------------

-Session-RPE for the workout should be 3-4/10

Very easy ride in Z1 or low end of Z2. Don't let heart rate exceed Z2 at any point. For power, take the midpoint of Z2 as your cap. Going harder will not make the ride any better/more beneficial, so hold yourself back!

Session-RPE for the workout should be 1-3/10.

The purpose of the ride is simply to collect more aerobic cycling volume with an absolute minimal impact on tissue (muscle, tendon and bone) and on the central nervous system.

Endurance ride in Z2 (keep both power AND heart rate in Z2).

This ride should be controlled and fairly comfortable but you shouldn't just be lollygagging. If training for non-draft races, ideally do this ride (as far as possible) in the TT position.

Session-RPE for the ride should be 2-4

Warm-up:
-30' easy, gradually getting towards the high end of Z2. Include 4 x 30" builds to FTP or higher.

Main set - note cadence prescriptions! You can do this workout sitting up if you find that it's difficult to pedal smoothly at low rpm:s when in the aerobars.

5 x (
-1.5' @ 110-115% FTP, 50-60 rpm (important!!!)
-3.5' @ 50-65% FTP, choice rpm
)

4 x (
-10' @ 80-85% FTP, 50-60 rpm (important!!!)
-5' @ 60-65% FTP, choice rpm
)

-Easy riding in Z2 to prescribed duration. Keep the effort nice and relaxed, stay at the low end of the zone if needed.

------------------------------------------------------

-Session-RPE for the workout should be 6-8/10

Endurance run in Z2 (keep both pace/power AND heart rate in Z2).

This run should be controlled and fairly comfortable but it shouldn't be a shuffle. Do the "talk test" (try talking to yourself and see if you can talk in complete sentences without huffing and puffing).

Session-RPE for the run should be 3-5

Warm-up:
-20' easy, gradually getting towards the high end of Z2. Include 4 x 30" builds - the first one at least to threshold pace/power/effort, and then gradually increase the effort of each subsequent build.

Main set - the workout should be hard (not too far from 'best effort'), but leave a few reps in the tank.

-15 x 60" @ ~3k race-pace / 60" very easy jog

-Easy running in Z2 or Z1 to prescribed duration.

------------------------------------------------------

-Do this run on a track, treadmill with 1% grade, or flat roads. If running on the track, feel free to translate the time-based prescription to an even distance-based prescription instead.

-Be well fuelled and hydrated coming into the workout. You might also benefit from having a gel/some sport drink during the workout.

-Session-RPE for the workout should be 8-9/10

Main set - note cadence prescriptions! You can do this workout sitting up if you find that it's difficult to pedal smoothly at low rpm:s when in the aerobars.

3 x (
-10' @ 60-65% FTP, choice rpm
-20' @ 70-75% FTP, 50-60 rpm (important!!!)
)

------------------------------------------------------

-Session-RPE for the workout should be 3-4/10

workoutList.workoutName: Knuckle Down (includes option for Zwift race)
workoutList.description:
This ride combines two important elements of "base training": high-cadence (neuromuscular) work in the first hour of the ride, low-cadence (strength endurance) work in the second hour, and in the final hour we do a bit of high-intensity maintenance while already fatigued to get a bit of an additional stimulus.

-1h @ 55-70% FTP that includes the following set:
4 x (
-10' @ self-selected cadence
-4' @ 100+ rpm (can be done sitting up)
-1' @ maximum sustainable cadence (can be done sitting up)
)

-1h as 3 x (15' @ 70-75% FTP and 50-60 rpm, 5' very easy spin)

-1h 15 min as
*4 x 2´ @ VO2max-effort (estimated best 5-minute power, or ~115-120% FTP) / 2' easy spin
*4' easy spin
*5 x 4' @ threshold-effort (95-100% FTP) / 1' easy spin
*5' easy spin
*Then easy riding in Z2 to prescribed duration. Keep the effort nice and relaxed, stay at the low end of the zone if needed.

------------------------------------------------------

-Session-RPE for the workout should be 7-9/10

-Be well fuelled and hydrated coming into...

Very easy run in Z1 or low end of Z2. Don't let heart rate exceed Z2 at any point. For pace/power, take the midpoint of Z2 as your cap. Going faster will not make the run any better/more beneficial, so hold yourself back!

Session-RPE for the workout should be 1-3/10.

The purpose of the run is simply to collect some running volume with an absolute minimal impact on tissue (muscle, tendon and bone) and on the central nervous system.

Endurance ride in Z2.

Include a 40-minute segment (can be done sitting up) as
4 x (
-4' @ 85+ rpm
-3' @ 95+ rpm
-2' @ 105+ rpm
-1' @ 115+ rpm
)

Session-RPE for the ride should be 3-4

Endurance run in Z2 (keep both pace/power AND heart rate in Z2). Include 12 x 15-sec fast strides focused on form, cadence, and relaxed speed after at least 15 minutes of running. At least 1 minute of easy jogging between strides.

Session-RPE for the run should be 3-5

-----------------------------------------

Strides are accelerations to ~95% of max speed with great running form. Work on great posture, high cadence, and a strong but relaxed leg and arm drive. Jog at a very easy pace between strides so you can do each with great form.

RELAX when running strides. Don't let your hands, face or arms tense up. You should NOT mistake strides for all out sprints. Some mantras to help you get strides right: "Fast but relaxed", "No strain", "Easy speed"

Warm-up:
-20' easy, gradually getting towards the high end of Z2. Include 4 x 30" builds to FTP or higher.

Main set - feel free to do this workout sitting up. Hitting the prescribed power targets is the main priority here, not specificity of bike position:
2 x (
-15' alternating 1.5' @ ~110% FTP, 1.5' @ ~75% FTP
-7 minutes easy spin
)

-Easy riding in Z2 to prescribed duration.

------------------------------------------------------

-Session-RPE for the workout should be 6-8/10

-Be well fuelled and hydrated coming into the workout. And fuel and hydrate adequately during the workout.

Very easy ride in Z1 or low end of Z2. Don't let heart rate exceed Z2 at any point. For power, take the midpoint of Z2 as your cap. Going harder will not make the ride any better/more beneficial, so hold yourself back!

Session-RPE for the workout should be 1-3/10.

The purpose of the ride is simply to collect more aerobic cycling volume with an absolute minimal impact on tissue (muscle, tendon and bone) and on the central nervous system.

Endurance run in Z2 (keep both pace/power AND heart rate in Z2). Include 8 x 15-sec fast strides focused on form, cadence, and relaxed speed after at least 15 minutes of running. At least 1 minute of easy jogging between strides.

Session-RPE for the run should be 2-4

-----------------------------------------

Strides are accelerations to ~95% of max speed with great running form. Work on great posture, high cadence, and a strong but relaxed leg and arm drive. Jog at a very easy pace between strides so you can do each with great form.

RELAX when running strides. Don't let your hands, face or arms tense up. You should NOT mistake strides for all out sprints. Some mantras to help you get strides right: "Fast but relaxed", "No strain", "Easy speed"

Warm-up:
-30' easy, gradually getting towards the high end of Z2. Include 4 x 30" builds to FTP or higher.

Main set - note cadence prescriptions! You can do this workout sitting up if you find that it's difficult to pedal smoothly at low rpm:s when in the aerobars.

6 x (
-1.5' @ 110-115% FTP, 50-60 rpm (important!!!)
-3.5' @ 50-65% FTP, choice rpm
)

6 x (
-10' @ 80-85% FTP, 50-60 rpm (important!!!)
-5' @ 60-65% FTP, choice rpm
)

-Easy riding in Z2 to prescribed duration. Keep the effort nice and relaxed, stay at the low end of the zone if needed.

------------------------------------------------------

-Session-RPE for the workout should be 6-8/10

Very easy ride in Z1 or low end of Z2. Don't let heart rate exceed Z2 at any point. For power, take the midpoint of Z2 as your cap. Going harder will not make the ride any better/more beneficial, so hold yourself back!

Session-RPE for the workout should be 1-3/10.

The purpose of the ride is simply to collect more aerobic cycling volume with an absolute minimal impact on tissue (muscle, tendon and bone) and on the central nervous system.

Very easy run in Z1 or low end of Z2. Don't let heart rate exceed Z2 at any point. For pace/power, take the midpoint of Z2 as your cap. Going faster will not make the run any better/more beneficial, so hold yourself back!

Session-RPE for the workout should be 1-3/10.

Session-RPE for the workout should be 1-3/10.

The purpose of the run is simply to collect some running volume with an absolute minimal impact on tissue (muscle, tendon and bone) and on the central nervous system.

Warm-up:
-15' easy, gradually getting towards the high end of Z2.

Main set - note cadence prescriptions! You can do this workout sitting up if you find that it's difficult to pedal smoothly at low rpm:s when in the aerobars.
5 x (
-10' @ 80-85% FTP, 50-60 rpm (important!!!)
-5' @ 60-65% FTP, choice rpm
)

------------------------------------------------------

-Session-RPE for the workout should be 5-7/10

Warm-up:
-30' easy, gradually getting towards the high end of Z2. Include 4 x 30" builds to FTP or higher.

Main set - if you can, do as much as possible in the aerobars if training for a non-draft race (but you can take short breaks from the TT-position and sit up every now and then if needed):

2 x (
-15 minutes @ 85-95% of FTP
-5 minutes easy spin
)
*The first of these two intervals @ 65-75 rpm

-5' easy spin
-30' @ ~70% FTP, 50-60 rpm
-5' easy spin

2 x (
-15 minutes @ 85-95% of FTP.
-5 minutes easy spin
)
*The first of these two intervals @ 65-75 rpm

-Easy riding in Z2 to prescribed duration.

------------------------------------------------------

-Session-RPE for the workout should be 6-8/10

-Be well fuelled and hydrated coming into the workout. And fuel and hydrate adequately during the workout.

Endurance run in Z2 (keep both pace/power AND heart rate in Z2). Include 10 x 10-sec all out hill sprints after at least 15 minutes of running. At least 1 minute of walking/standing recovery between hill sprints.

Session-RPE for the run should be 2-4

-----------------------------------------

Hill sprints are short, maximum efforts that aim to improve neuromuscular aspects of running (like force production, rate of force development, and motor unit recruitment). Check your watch during the first sprint to get yourself a start and endpoint. Then simply sprint from that start to endpoint for all subsequent sprints.

Focus on great posture, strong leg and knee drive and arm action, keep your head high and gaxe up, keep up a great cadence, and minimise ground contact time.

Walk slowly back to the start point after the sprint and recover standing until the start of the next sprint.

Main set - note cadence prescriptions! You can do this workout sitting up if you find that it's difficult to pedal smoothly at low rpm:s when in the aerobars.

6 x (
-5' @ 60-65% FTP, choice rpm
-15' @ 70-75% FTP, 50-60 rpm (important!!!)
)

------------------------------------------------------

-Session-RPE for the workout should be 3-4/10

Very easy ride in Z1 or low end of Z2. Don't let heart rate exceed Z2 at any point. For power, take the midpoint of Z2 as your cap. Going harder will not make the ride any better/more beneficial, so hold yourself back!

Session-RPE for the workout should be 1-3/10.

The purpose of the ride is simply to collect more aerobic cycling volume with an absolute minimal impact on tissue (muscle, tendon and bone) and on the central nervous system.

Session-RPE for the workout should be 1-3/10.

Endurance ride in Z2.

Throughout the ride, alternate between
-7' @ self-selected cadence
-2' @ 100+ rpm (can be done sitting up)
-1' @ 110 + rpm (can be done sitting up)

Session-RPE for the ride should be 3-4

)

)

-Easy riding in Z2 to prescribed duration. Keep the effort nice and relaxed, stay at the low end of the zone if needed.

------------------------------------------------------

Main set:

------------------------------------------------------

Warm up, run and walk with strong effort, cool down.

workoutSteps.description: Run strong. Walk if you need to.

workoutList.description: Warm up, cadence drills, acceleration-glider drills, speed drills, cool down.

workoutSteps.description:
Cadence Drill
Feet low to the ground, light touch! You can always improve by one more step.

Acceleration-Glider Drill
Transition smoothly from walk to jog to run, then glide seamlessly back to a walk.

workoutList.description: Warm up, cadence drills, acceleration-glider drills, Magic Mile, race pace repeats, cool down.

workoutSteps.description:
Cadence Drill
Feet low to the ground, light touch! You can always improve by one more step.

Acceleration-Glider Drill
Transition smoothly from walk to jog to run, then glide seamlessly back to a walk.

Magic Mile
Push yourself, but don't sprint. Don't start too fast.

workoutList.description: Warm up, cadence drills, acceleration-glider drills, hill repeats, cool down.

workoutSteps.description:
Cadence Drill
Feet low to the ground, light touch! You can always improve by one more step.

Acceleration-Glider Drill
Transition smoothly from walk to jog to run, then glide seamlessly back to a walk.
Get in position: Bottom of hill.

Uphill
Shorten your stride and increase cadence up the hill.

Downhill
Run down with a light touch, feet low.
